How to watch Brighton vs Wolves
arts.sum: Brighton vs Wolves kicks off at 7.45pm with the Matchday Live stream beginning around 30 minutes ahead of kick-off at 7.15pm. Wolves continue their Carabao Cup campaign with a third round match away to Brighton & Hove Albion on Wednesday evening - find out how to follow the live action below.

arts.ttl: 'I don't like it': Referee Chris Kavanagh told Declan Rice he did NOT want to send him off against Brighton but Howard Webb explains why the Arsenal star was correctly shown red
arts.sum: Rice was issued with a yellow card in the first half for a late challenge on Joel Veltman, who was also involved in the incident that resulted in Rice receiving his second booking after half-time.

arts.ttl: Match Officials Mic'd Up: Webb analyses key incidents in MW1-3
arts.sum: What the match officials did: Referee Chris Kavanagh spots that Rice kicks the ball away before Veltman catches him, he then issues Rice a second yellow card for delaying the restart of play. What the match officials did: The assistant referee says the ball strikes Kovacic but believes his arm is in a natural position and the ball comes at him at speed.
